findings.message: Unsanitized input from the request URL flows into django.http.response.HttpResponse, where it is used to render an HTML page returned to the user. This may result in a Cross-Site Scripting attack (XSS).

Unsanitized input from an HTTP parameter flows into flask.render_template_string, where it is used to construct a template that gets rendered. This may result in a Server-Side Template Injection vulnerability.

Unsanitized input from an HTTP parameter flows into flask.render_template_string, where it is used to construct a template that gets rendered. This may result in a Server-Side Template Injection vulnerability.
Unsanitized input from an HTTP parameter flows into the return value of document, where it is used to render an HTML page returned to the user. This may result in a Cross-Site Scripting attack (XSS).

Unsanitized input from an HTTP parameter flows into the return value of statement, where it is used to render an HTML page returned to the user. This may result in a Cross-Site Scripting attack (XSS).
Unsanitized input from an HTTP parameter flows into flask.render_template_string, where it is used to render an HTML page returned to the user. This may result in a Cross-Site Scripting attack (XSS).
Unsanitized input from an HTTP parameter flows into flask.render_template_string, where it is used to render an HTML page returned to the user. This may result in a Cross-Site Scripting attack (XSS).
Unsanitized input from an HTTP header flows into flask.redirect, where it is used as input for request redirection. This may result in an Open Redirect vulnerability.
Unsanitized input from an HTTP parameter flows into flask.send_file, where it is used as a path. This may result in a Path Traversal vulnerability and allow an attacker to read arbitrary files.
Unsanitized input from an environment variable flows into eval, where it is executed as Python code. This may result in a Code Injection vulnerability.

Unsanitized input from an opened tar file flows into extractall, where it is used to extract files from a tar archive. This may result files outside destination directory to be overwritten, resulting in an arbitrary file write.

Unsanitized input from a web form flows into the return value of create_restore_job, where it is used to render an HTML page returned to the user. This may result in a Cross-Site Scripting attack (XSS).

Unsanitized input from an HTTP parameter flows into the return value of run_subscription_now, where it is used to render an HTML page returned to the user. This may result in a Cross-Site Scripting attack (XSS).
Unsanitized input from an HTTP parameter flows into execute, where it is used in an SQL query. This may result in an SQL Injection vulnerability.

xml.etree.ElementTree.fromstring is vulnerable to XXE and DDOS in versions of python <= 3.10. If using python runtime 3.10 or older, consider upgrading python versions, or see https://docs.python.org/3.10/library/xml.html#xml-vulnerabilities for mitigations. Python versions 3.11 and onwards do not have this issue.

xml.etree.ElementTree.fromstring is vulnerable to XXE and DDOS in versions of python <= 3.10. If using python runtime 3.10 or older, consider upgrading python versions, or see https://docs.python.org/3.10/library/xml.html#xml-vulnerabilities for mitigations. Python versions 3.11 and onwards do not have this issue.
